channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Russian court sentences US citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage
item.description: A Russian court has sentenced a U.S. citizen to 15 years in jail for espionage on Tuesday, according to Russian state media outlets. Eugene Spector, who was born in Russia but later moved to the U.S. and got his U.S. citizenship, was already serving a 3 1/2-year sentence for bribery.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: US and UK criticize Pakistani military court convictions of civilian supporters of Imran Khan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The United States and the United Kingdom have expressed concern over convictions imposed by Pakistani military courts to 25 civilian supporters of former Prime Minister Imran Khan over their alleged involvement in riots last year.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: France’s president and prime minister have managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Angela Charlton, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rahm Emanuel rips US defense firms, calls for punishments in final Tokyo missive
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Japan Rahm Emanuel is calling for defense firms that miss deadlines and increase costs to be forbidden from bidding on new military contracts as a punishment.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN investigative team says Syria's new authorities 'very receptive' to probe of Assad war crimes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. organization assisting in investigating the most serious crimes in Syria said Monday the country’s new authorities were “very receptive” to its request for cooperation during a just-concluded visit to Damascus, and it is preparing to deploy.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military carries out airstrike in Syria, killing 2 ISIS operatives
item.description: The U.S. military conducted an airstrike on Monday in Syria, where they killed a pair of ISIS operatives, wounded another and destroyed a truckload of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mulvaney on Trump, Panama Canal: It’s a negotiation
item.description: Former White House chief of staff Mick Mulvaney said Monday that President-elect Trump's recent provocations about the U.S. possibly retaking control of the Panama Canal are part of a negotiating tactic to get the rates down.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO leaders predict era of 2% defense spending 'probably history' as Trump reportedly floats higher target
item.description: European leaders are making clear ahead of the Trump presidency that they plan to spend more on defense as Russia remains a top threat and as security concerns in the Middle East and North Africa escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland to Trump: We're not for sale
item.description: Greenland’s prime minister said Monday the autonomous island is not for sale after President-elect Trump said owning Greenland “is an absolute necessity” for the U.S. “Greenland is ours.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Holy Year is about to start in Rome. Here's what you need to know
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday formally inaugurates the 2025 Holy Year, reviving an ancient church tradition encouraging the faithful to make pilgrimages to Rome, amid new security fears following a Christmas market attack in Germany.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's Putin holds talks with Slovakian PM Fico, in a rare visit to Moscow by an EU leader
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Sunday hosted Slovakia’s prime minister, Robert Fico, in a rare visit to the Kremlin by an EU leader since Moscow's all-out invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 10 are killed as a small plane crashes into a Brazilian town popular with tourists
item.description: A small plane crashed into a Brazilian town that's popular with tourists on Sunday, killing all 10 passengers on board and injuring more than a dozen people on the ground, Brazil's Civil Defense Agency said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Four women and a 9-year-old boy were killed and 200 people were injured when a man drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg — an attack that has left Germans mourning the victims and with a shaken sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Projectile from Yemen strikes near Tel Aviv, injuring more than a dozen: officials
item.description: A projectile launched into Israel from Yemen overnight struck Tel Aviv, leading to 16 people suffering mild injuries, according to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power after key ally vows to topple him
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power next year after NDP leader Jagmeet Singh said he would move to bring down the minority Liberal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran expands weaponization capabilities critical for employing nuclear bomb
item.description: A new report presented by the National Council of Resistance of Iran claims Tehran has expanded attempts to secretly develop weaponization capabilities for its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
